title: RFC 9309 Robots Semantics and Library Conformance Adapter · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Construct the RFC 9309 retrieval URL from the target service origin and send one stable truthful crawler product token with an accountable contact.
Apply group selection, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior from RFC 9309.
Classify successful, redirecting, unavailable, unreachable, oversized, malformed, and stale robots responses conservatively.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Robots Location Agent
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 7578 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove RFC 9309 product, group, encoding, longest-match, response-state, cache, and library adapter behavior; owner authorization and revocation must remain separate from robots and legal-review claims; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Construct the RFC 9309 retrieval URL from the target service origin and send one stable truthful crawler product token with an accountable contact.

Inspect the Robots Location Agent trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct the RFC 9309 retrieval URL from the target service origin and send one stable truthful crawler product token with an accountable contact. Artifact under review: RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Robots Location Agent, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Robots Location Agent record says RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Robots Location Agent.

For inspect the failure record, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Robots Location Agent for RFC 9309 decision adapter around robots-parser.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Extension Restraint
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-extension-restraint-dpdaqg" that uses case 4042 to prove RFC 9309 product, group, encoding, longest-match, response-state, cache, and library adapter behavior. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Extension Restraint.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Extension Restraint into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Extension Restraint, keep Extension Restraint connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
